truth.papers.A11.title: Quantification of multi-stage recrystallization in low-alloy steel under varying deformation conditions using inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D cellular automaton
truth.papers.A11.paper_scope: Develop an inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D cellular automaton model to quantify multi-stage dynamic recrystallization and flow stress evolution in low-alloy steel under varying deformation conditions.

prediction.papers.A11.title: Quantification of multi-stage recrystallization in low-alloy steel under varying deformation conditions using inhomogeneous-dislocation-density 3D cellular automaton
prediction.papers.A11.paper_scope: Development and experimental validation of a 3D cellular automaton model incorporating inhomogeneous dislocation density and grain-boundary sliding to predict multi-stage dynamic recrystallization microstructure and flow stress of low-alloy steel under transient strain rate and temperature variations during hot compression.

prediction.alloys.alloy_04c.alloys_notes: Material mainly applied for the production of automobile hub bearings, provided in the form of hot rolled round bars.

truth.processes.proc_850_001_const.description: Hot compression at constant temperature 850°C and strain rate 0.01 s^-1 to true strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_850_001_const.processes_n: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 850°C at 10°C/s, held 60 s, compressed, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_850_1_const.description: Hot compression at constant temperature 850°C and strain rate 1 s^-1 to true strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_850_1_const.processes_not: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 850°C at 10°C/s, held 60 s, compressed, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_001_const.description: Hot compression at constant temperature 950°C and strain rate 0.01 s^-1 to true strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_001_const.processes_n: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C at 10°C/s, held 60 s, compressed, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_01_const.description: Hot compression at constant temperature 950°C and strain rate 0.1 s^-1 to true strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_01_const.processes_no: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C at 10°C/s, held 60 s, compressed, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_001_to_01_02.descript: Varying strain rate: first stage at 950°C, strain rate 0.01 s^-1 to strain 0.2, then transiently increased to 0.1 s^-1 and compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_001_to_01_02.processe: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C, held 60 s; first stage to ε=0.2 at 0.01 s^-1, second stage at 0.1 s^-1 to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_001_to_01_04.descript: Varying strain rate: first stage at 950°C, strain rate 0.01 s^-1 to strain 0.4, then transiently increased to 0.1 s^-1 and compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_001_to_01_04.processe: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C, held 60 s; first stage to ε=0.4 at 0.01 s^-1, second stage at 0.1 s^-1 to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_01_to_001_02.descript: Varying strain rate: first stage at 950°C, strain rate 0.1 s^-1 to strain 0.2, then transiently decreased to 0.01 s^-1 and compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_01_to_001_02.processe: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C, held 60 s; first stage to ε=0.2 at 0.1 s^-1, second stage at 0.01 s^-1 to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_01_to_001_04.descript: Varying strain rate: first stage at 950°C, strain rate 0.1 s^-1 to strain 0.4, then transiently decreased to 0.01 s^-1 and compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_01_to_001_04.processe: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C, held 60 s; first stage to ε=0.4 at 0.1 s^-1, second stage at 0.01 s^-1 to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_1050_001_const.descriptio: Hot compression at constant temperature 1050°C and strain rate 0.01 s^-1 to true strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_1050_001_const.processes_: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 1050°C at 10°C/s, held 60 s, compressed, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_1050_to_950_02.descriptio: Varying temperature: strain rate 0.01 s^-1, initial temperature 1050°C up to strain 0.2, then continuously decreased to 950°C in strain range 0.2-0.3, compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_1050_to_950_02.processes_: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 1050°C, held 60 s; deformed at 1050°C to ε=0.2, then temperature decreased to 950°C over Δε=0.1, further compressed to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_1050_to_950_04.descriptio: Varying temperature: strain rate 0.01 s^-1, initial temperature 1050°C up to strain 0.4, then continuously decreased to 950°C in strain range 0.4-0.5, compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_1050_to_950_04.processes_: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 1050°C, held 60 s; deformed at 1050°C to ε=0.4, then temperature decreased to 950°C over Δε=0.1, further compressed to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_to_1050_02.descriptio: Varying temperature: strain rate 0.01 s^-1, initial temperature 950°C up to strain 0.2, then continuously increased to 1050°C in strain range 0.2-0.3, compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_to_1050_02.processes_: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C, held 60 s; deformed at 950°C to ε=0.2, then temperature increased to 1050°C over Δε=0.1, further compressed to ε=0.9, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_950_to_1050_04.descriptio: Varying temperature: strain rate 0.01 s^-1, initial temperature 950°C up to strain 0.4, then continuously increased to 1050°C in strain range 0.4-0.5, compressed to total strain 0.9.
truth.processes.proc_950_to_1050_04.processes_: Heated to 1250°C at 10°C/s, held 180 s, cooled to 950°C, held 60 s; deformed at 950°C to ε=0.4, then temperature increased to 1050°C over Δε=0.1, further compressed to ε=0.9, water quenched.

prediction.processes.proc_low_alloy_init.descriptio: As-received hot rolled round bar; initial mixed-grain prior austenite microstructure characterized without experimental thermomechanical treatment.
prediction.processes.proc_950_01_const.description: Constant deformation hot compression: shared pre-heating to 1250 °C at 10 °C/s, holding 180 s, cooling to deformation temperature at 10 °C/s, holding 60 s, compressing to true strain 0.9 under constant temperature and strain rate, immediate water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_950_01_const.processes_no: Deformation temperatures 850-1150 °C; strain rates 0.01-10 s⁻¹. Cylindrical specimens of φ6 mm × 9 mm with 0.1 mm tantalum foil at interfaces.
prediction.processes.proc_950_01_to_001_04.descript: Varying strain rate hot compression at 950 °C: first stage at constant strain rate (0.01 or 0.1 s⁻¹) to fixed strains 0.2 or 0.4, followed by transient increase or decrease to 0.1 or 0.01 s⁻¹, compressing to total true strain 0.9, water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_950_01_to_001_04.processe: Shared pre-heating and cooling sequence identical to constant deformation process.
prediction.processes.proc_1050_to_950_04.descriptio: Varying temperature hot compression at 0.01 s⁻¹: first stage at 1050 °C or 950 °C, then continuous decrease or increase to 950 °C or 1050 °C in strain ranges 0.2-0.3 or 0.4-0.5, compressing to total true strain 0.9, water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_1050_to_950_04.processes_: Shared pre-heating and cooling sequence identical to constant deformation process.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_850_001_const.pr: Immediate water quenching after compression to freeze high-temperature microstructure.

truth.structures.sample_850_001_const.overall_: Partially recrystallized prior austenite microstructure after hot compression at 850°C, 0.01 s^-1, water quenched (martensite). Experimental DRX fraction ~79.3%, average grain size 34.01 ± 4.71 µm (side section).
truth.structures.sample_850_001_const.microstr: mixed: elongated unrecrystallized grains and equiaxed recrystallized grains
34.01 µm (average, experimental side section), CA 3D: 34.53 µm
assumed initial 1e11 m^-2 uniform, inhomogeneous during deformation
experimental side section 34.01 ± 4.71 µm, CA 2D: 32.94 µm
truth.structures.sample_850_1_const.overall_st: Partially recrystallized prior austenite microstructure after hot compression at 850°C, 1 s^-1. Experimental DRX fraction ~42.8%, average grain size 61.38 ± 7.41 µm (side section).
truth.structures.sample_850_1_const.microstruc: mixed, elongated matrix and few recrystallized grains
truth.structures.sample_950_001_const.overall_: Initial mixed-grain microstructure (as-homogenized at 1250°C) used as starting condition for all samples. Maximum grain size 265.20 µm, minimum 10.22 µm, average grain size in 2D ~140.01 µm (optical measurements).
truth.structures.sample_950_001_const.microstr: average 140.01 µm (2D) / 143.78 µm (3D), max 265.20 µm, min 10.22 µm
SD 51.88 µm (2D), Max 265.20 µm, Min 10.22 µm

prediction.structures.sample_low_alloy_init.overall: Initial mixed-grain prior austenite microstructure in as-quenched condition, exhibiting significant grain size heterogeneity.
prediction.structures.sample_low_alloy_init.microst: Right-skewed; 2D AGS 140.01 μm (SD 51.88 μm), min 7.46 μm, max 270.78 μm; 3D AGS 143.78 μm (SD 58.25 μm), min 6.45 μm, max 283.29 μm; metallographic frequency peaks at 80-120 μm.
prediction.structures.sample_850_001_const.overall_: Partially recrystallized microstructure after hot compression at 850 °C and 0.01 s⁻¹; elongated matrix remains alongside fine DRX grains.
prediction.structures.sample_850_001_const.microstr: Side section 34.01 ± 4.71 μm; 2D YZ surface 32.94 μm
inhomogeneous distribution; higher near grain boundaries and in coarse grains
Experimental side-section average; DRX fraction approximately 76.4% on YZ surface at 0.9 strain.
prediction.structures.sample_850_1_const.overall_st: Partially recrystallized microstructure after hot compression at 850 °C and 1 s⁻¹; lower DRX fraction and larger remaining matrix grains compared to 0.01 s⁻¹.
prediction.structures.sample_850_1_const.microstruc: DRX grains equiaxed; matrix elongated and coarse
Side section 61.38 ± 7.41 μm; 2D YZ surface 59.08 μm
inhomogeneous; higher in matrix due to limited DRX
Experimental side-section average at 0.9 strain; DRX fraction approximately 38.7% on YZ surface.
prediction.structures.sample_950_001_const.overall_: Nearly fully recrystallized microstructure after constant compression at 950 °C and 0.01 s⁻¹.
prediction.structures.sample_950_001_const.microstr: Constant condition baseline for decreasing-Zp comparisons at 0.9 strain.
prediction.structures.sample_950_01_const.overall_s: Fully recrystallized fine microstructure after constant compression at 950 °C and 0.1 s⁻¹.
prediction.structures.sample_950_01_const.microstru: Constant condition baseline for increasing-Zp comparisons at 0.9 strain.
prediction.structures.sample_950_001_to_01_04.overa: Multi-stage DRX microstructure after transiently increasing strain rate (0.01 to 0.1 s⁻¹) at 0.4 strain; larger final AGS than constant 0.1 s⁻¹ due to delayed DRXGsII nucleation and residual DRXGsI.
prediction.structures.sample_950_001_to_01_04.micro: Fine DRXGsII refining residual DRXGsI and matrix
Final AGS 37.12 μm; DRXGsI 42.46 μm, DRXGsII 33.73 μm
inhomogeneous; DRXGsII lower than DRXGsI and matrix
DRXGsI volume fraction 0.34; DRXGsII volume fraction 0.64 at 0.9 strain.
prediction.structures.sample_950_01_to_001_04.overa: Multi-stage DRX microstructure after transiently decreasing strain rate (0.1 to 0.01 s⁻¹) at 0.4 strain; final AGS similar to constant 0.01 s⁻¹ because DRXGsI grow rapidly and DRXGsII quickly reach constant-condition size.
prediction.structures.sample_950_01_to_001_04.micro: DRXGsI and DRXGsII both equiaxed, similar sizes
Final AGS 52.58 μm; DRXGsI 46.11 μm, DRXGsII 54.32 μm
inhomogeneous; residual matrix dislocation density higher than under constant conditions transiently after drop
DRXGsI volume fraction 0.24; DRXGsII volume fraction 0.76 at 0.9 strain.
prediction.structures.sample_950_to_1050_04.overall: Multi-stage DRX microstructure after transiently increasing temperature (950 to 1050 °C) at 0.01 s⁻¹; initial refinement then slight coarsening, final AGS lower than constant 1050 °C.
prediction.structures.sample_950_to_1050_04.microst: Refined then slightly coarsened equiaxed DRX grains
Experimental final AGS approximately 70 μm (0.4 strain transient)
inhomogeneous; matrix retains higher dislocation density transiently after temperature rise
Fig. 16d indicates experimental AGS at 0.9 strain ~70 μm for εI=0.4; simulation shows initial refinement to ~62.63 μm at 0.5 strain then coarsening.
prediction.structures.sample_1050_to_950_04.overall: Multi-stage DRX microstructure after transiently decreasing temperature (1050 to 950 °C) at 0.01 s⁻¹; final AGS larger than constant 950 °C due to lower DRX fraction growth in second stage.
prediction.structures.sample_1050_to_950_04.microst: Residual coarse DRXGsI alongside finer DRXGsII
inhomogeneous; delayed nucleation of DRXGsII at lower temperature
Fig. 16c indicates experimental AGS at 0.9 strain ~60 μm for εI=0.4; DRX fraction evolution slower than constant 950 °C.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_850_001_: EBSD (SU5000 SEM, step size 0.65-1.3 µm) and ARPGE reconstruction
Reconstruct prior austenite grains from martensite, distinguish recrystallized grains using GOS threshold 3°
Reconstructed grain size agreed with optical measurement; maps showed incomplete recrystallization
truth.characterization_methods.sample_850_1_co: Reconstruct PAGs and identify DRX grains
Consistent with optical results; low recrystallization fraction
truth.characterization_methods.sample_950_001_: Max grain size 265.20 µm, min 10.22 µm, 2D average 140.01 µm

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_low_allo: Characterize initial prior austenite grain morphology and size distribution before deformation
Mixed-grain microstructure with 2D AGS 140.01 μm (SD 51.88 μm) and 3D AGS 143.78 μm (SD 58.25 μm); right-skewed distribution peaking at 80-120 μm.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_850_001_: Observe deformed and quenched microstructure at side sections
Elonated matrix still visible at 850 °C / 0.01 s⁻¹ despite DRX; AGS 34.01 ± 4.71 μm at 0.9 strain.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_850_1_co: Observe deformed and quenched microstructure at side sections
Coarser microstructure at 850 °C / 1 s⁻¹; AGS 61.38 ± 7.41 μm at 0.9 strain; lower DRX fraction due to high strain rate limiting nucleation and growth.

truth.computational_details.sample_950_001_con: 3D cellular automaton with inhomogeneous dislocation density, incorporating dislocation gradients and grain-boundary sliding
In-house CA code, DEFORM 3D with Fortran user subroutines

Flow stress curves, DRX fraction evolutions, and average grain sizes compared with hot compression experiments under constant and varying conditions; RMSE of flow stress 3.3118 MPa, R² 0.9589; AGS RMSE 4.0169 µm, R² 0.9777; DRX fraction RMSE 0.0229, R² 0.9784.
Modified phenomenological constitutive model (WH+DRV+DRX) with stress softening coefficient k_s and varying t_0.5
Calibrate dislocation accumulation/annihilation parameters and characteristic stresses for CA model; predict flow stress under varying conditions.
Incorporates effect of DRX history on saturation stress and time for 50% DRX fraction; validated by FEM.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: 3D Cellular Automaton with inhomogeneous dislocation density and grain-boundary sliding
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Under varying deformation conditions: flow stress RMSE 3.3118 MPa, R² 0.9589, AARE 4.57%; AGS RMSE 4.0169 μm, R² 0.9777, AARE 4.11%; DRX fraction RMSE 0.0229, R² 0.9784, AARE 2.47%. Under constant conditions: flow stress RMSE 4.8944 MPa, R² 0.9909, AARE 3.91%.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Calibrate characteristic stresses, varying t0.5, and softening coefficient ks for flow stress prediction under transient Zp changes

GA-calibrated parameters in Table 1: mu0 7.89e10 MPa, Tm 1800 K, KT0 680.912, QKT 23134.8, psi 9.92e-3, q 0.867, Qdrx 326922 J/mol, Qp 314300 J/mol, A 7.3214e11, alpha 0.0089, n 6.3742, kd0 4.442, Qkd 728.468, nAv0 18.097, QAv 21895.1, nc 55.563
Compared to Model 1 (original), Model 2 improves prediction under varying conditions: R² 0.9507 vs 0.8239, RMSE 3.0399 MPa vs 6.4554 MPa, AARE 3.11% vs 7.36%.
Describe average dislocation accumulation and dynamic recovery
k1 (average WH coefficient), k2 (average DRV coefficient), rho_sat (saturation dislocation density)
Integrated into CA to compute average dislocation density evolution; local inhomogeneity introduced via Eq. (28)-(32).
Account for strain contribution from GBS in fine grains
Reduces dislocation slipping strain increment in smaller grains and DRX grains, influencing inhomogeneous dislocation distribution.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Figure 7 presents experimental and simulated flow stress–strain curves at 850, 950, 1050, 1150 °C and strain rates 0.01–10 s⁻¹; specific yield/peak stress values are not digitized in the text, only curves are shown.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 16 shows evolution of average grain size with transient variations; exact numerical values are not reported in the caption but indicate trends of grain size change.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Tables 6 and 7 provide simulated final grain sizes and volume fractions of first- and second-stage DRX grains under varying conditions; experimental counterparts are shown in figures but not tabulated.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The paper focuses on hot compression flow behavior, not on tensile properties; therefore no yield strength, UTS, or elongation values are reported in standard mechanical property format.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: EBSD maps are used to reconstruct prior austenite grains and assess recrystallization fraction, with GOS threshold of 3°.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Table 2 accuracy of modified constitutive model under constant conditions: flow stress RMSE 4.8944 MPa, R² 0.9909, AARE 3.91%; load RMSE 274.6820 N, R² 0.9918, AARE 6.65%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Table 3 comparison of Model 1 and Model 2 under varying conditions: Model 2 RMSE 3.0399 MPa, R² 0.9507, AARE 3.11%; Model 1 RMSE 6.4554 MPa, R² 0.8239, AARE 7.36%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Fig. 6 experimental and simulated DRX fraction evolution under constant conditions shows DRX fraction increases with higher temperature and lower strain rate; final DRX fraction approaches 1 at 1150 °C / 0.01 s⁻¹ but is delayed at 850 °C / 10 s⁻¹.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fig. 7 flow stress-strain curves under constant conditions: experimental and simulated curves at 850 °C, 950 °C, 1050 °C, 1150 °C with strain rates 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10 s⁻¹. High strain rate (10 s⁻¹) curves exhibit upwarp due to friction and dominant work hardening.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fig. 8 flow stress under transient strain rate variation at 950 °C: increasing strain rate (0.01 to 0.1 s⁻¹) at 0.2 or 0.4 strain produces lower peak and steady-state stress than constant 0.1 s⁻¹; decreasing strain rate (0.1 to 0.01 s⁻¹) drops stress below constant 0.01 s⁻¹ level within ~0.04 strain. Model 2 captures these trends better than Model 1.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 9 flow stress under transient temperature variation at 0.01 s⁻¹: decreasing temperature (1050 to 950 °C) produces a new lower peak in second stage; increasing temperature (950 to 1050 °C) causes rapid stress drop below constant 1050 °C level. Model 2 again outperforms Model 1.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Fig. 10 force-stroke comparisons under varying conditions confirm Model 2 predicts load evolution more accurately than Model 1, which overestimates second-stage force after strain-rate or temperature drops.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Fig. 11 simulated dislocation density distributions at 850 °C illustrate preferential DRX nucleation at grain boundaries of large grains due to strain localization; DRX grains accumulate lower dislocation density than matrix, causing stress softening.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Fig. 12a at 850 °C / 0.01 s⁻¹ and 0.9 strain: experimental side-section AGS 34.01 ± 4.71 μm, 2D YZ surface 32.94 μm, DRX fraction 76.4%. Fig. 12b at 850 °C / 1 s⁻¹ and 0.9 strain: experimental side-section AGS 61.38 ± 7.41 μm, 2D YZ surface 59.08 μm, DRX fraction 38.7%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Fig. 13 CA-predicted flow stress under varying conditions matches experimental peak stress and stress decline after increasing Zp, and agrees better than Model 2 on reduction velocity after decreasing Zp.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Fig. 14 DRX fraction evolution simulated by CA and Model 2 under varying Zp: after increasing Zp, DRX fraction temporarily stalls then rises via DRXGsI growth, remaining below constant condition in latter stage. After decreasing Zp, CA predicts DRX fraction exceeds constant condition due to rapid DRXGsI growth and matrix refinement, unlike Model 2.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Fig. 16 experimental and simulated AGS at 0.9 strain under varying conditions: (a) 950 °C 0.01→0.1 s⁻¹ ~50 μm; (b) 950 °C 0.1→0.01 s⁻¹ ~50 μm; (c) 1050→950 °C ~60 μm; (d) 950→1050 °C ~80 μm (εI=0.2) or ~70 μm (εI=0.4).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: Fig. 17 experimental and simulated microstructure under increasing strain rate at 950 °C: DRX fractions exceed 94% under both constant and varying conditions; residual DRXGsI (green) remain more with increasing εI, raising final AGS.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_14.value: Fig. 18a DRX fraction after increasing strain rate at 0.4 strain: experimental 75.7% at 0.4 strain, 84.6% at 0.5 strain, 95.8% at 0.9 strain; simulated 76.9%, 85.9%, 97.9%. Fig. 18b DRX fraction after decreasing temperature at 0.4 strain: first experimental group 91.4% at 0.4 strain, 96.3% at 0.5 strain, 98% at 0.9 strain; second experimental group 75.7% at 0.4 strain, 97.9% at 0.5 strain, 99% at 0.9 strain (corresponding simulated values 94.3/95.4/98.6 and 76.9/99.5/99.8).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_15.value: Fig. 19 microstructure under decreasing temperature (1050 to 950 °C) at 0.01 s⁻¹: final AGS larger than constant 950 °C; slight refinement at end of temperature drop (0.5 strain) but AGS remains significantly higher.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_16.value: Fig. 20 microstructure under decreasing strain rate (0.1 to 0.01 s⁻¹) at 0.4 strain: rapid transition to nearly full recrystallization within 0.1 strain; final AGS slightly lower than constant 0.01 s⁻¹.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_17.value: Fig. 21 microstructure under increasing temperature (950 to 1050 °C) at 0.01 s⁻¹: AGS decreases slightly from 65.33 μm at 0.4 strain to 62.63 μm at 0.5 strain, then coarsens; final AGS lower than constant 1050 °C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_18.value: Fig. 22 simulated DRX fraction and average dislocation density after increasing Zp: DRX fraction in second stage initially relies on DRXGsI growth (pseudo-metadynamic recrystallization); DRXGsII nucleation delayed by elevated critical dislocation density. Average dislocation density of DRXGsII is lower than both matrix and DRXGsI, driving refinement of both.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_19.value: Fig. 23 simulated microstructure and dislocation density on YZ surface after increasing Zp: DRXGsII nucleate at matrix grain boundaries first; at higher strains they refine DRXGsI due to dislocation density differences.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_20.value: Fig. 24 simulated DRX fraction and average dislocation density after decreasing Zp: enhanced DRV and rapid DRX grain refinement lower matrix dislocation density quickly; DRXGsI fraction initially rises then declines as DRXGsII refine them.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_21.value: Fig. 25 simulated microstructure after decreasing strain rate at 0.4 strain: DRXGsI grow rapidly, but matrix grains A/B/C retain higher dislocation density than under constant 0.01 s⁻¹ at 0.42 strain, promoting further DRX growth and higher final DRX fraction.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_22.value: Fig. 26 simulated microstructure after increasing temperature: despite total DRX fraction approaching constant 1050 °C level at 0.23 strain, matrix grains A/B/C still exhibit higher dislocation density, promoting rapid DRX growth to near-complete recrystallization by 0.3 strain.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_23.value: Fig. 27 simulated AGS evolutions of DRXGsI and DRXGsII under varying conditions: after increasing Zp, DRXGsI remain larger than DRXGsII and their volume fraction rises with εI. After decreasing Zp, DRXGsI grow rapidly to near-constant-condition size and are soon refined by DRXGsII.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_24.value: Table 5 3D CA accuracy under varying deformation conditions: flow stress RMSE 3.3118 MPa, R² 0.9589, AARE 4.57%; AGS RMSE 4.0169 μm, R² 0.9777, AARE 4.11%; DRX fraction RMSE 0.0229, R² 0.9784, AARE 2.47%.
